customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, cooperative posture. John is a first-time solar engager who typically declines solicitations — he chose to listen this time, which signals genuine curiosity. No skepticism or hostility observed. Primary vulnerability: physical disability (handicapped, mobility-limited) disclosed unprompted.
unresolved_concerns: What motivated John to engage this time after typically declining solar solicitations — never probed, unknown to rep or closer
Bill collection not completed — usage history not captured, system sizing unknown
Callback is unanchored — no confirmed time beyond 'about fifteen minutes,' no date, no locked appointment structure
sensitive_topics_raised: John disclosed he is handicapped and has limited mobility — mentioned in context of needing time to get into his house
exact_promises_rep_made: Rep will call John back in approximately fifteen minutes
Rep implied the program 'takes care of all the installation cost' — unanchored claim the closer should be aware of and prepared to clarify
closer_should_know: John is cooperative and bill-ready (he said 'I'll have the bills out and ready') but the callback has no structural protection — if the rep's callback doesn't land, there is no appointment on the books. Closer should treat this as a warm but fragile lead: John's motivation for engaging after typically declining is unknown and should be the first thing the closer surfaces, as it is the personalization hook for the entire proposal conversation.
